On this page

RxJS Wiki — Content Index ​

Catalog of all wiki pages. The LLM reads this first when answering queries. Updated on every ingest or page addition.

Overview ​

  • RxJS Overview — High-level synthesis: what RxJS is, core mental model, operator families, architecture role, key pitfalls

Core ​

RxJS primitives and building blocks.

  • Observable — Lazy push-based collection; cold vs hot; marble notation; pipe() composition
  • Observer — Consumer interface (next/error/complete); SafeSubscriber guarantees
  • Subscription — Handle to a running Observable; teardown logic; memory leak prevention
  • Subject — Multicast Observable + Observer; action bus; cold→hot bridge
  • Subjects — Complete Guide — All four variants compared; three roles; choosing guide; pitfalls; Subject vs shareReplay
  • BehaviorSubject — Subject with current value; state container; .value accessor
  • ReplaySubject — Subject with N-value replay buffer; late-join streams; cache
  • AsyncSubject — Emits only last value on complete; Promise analog
  • Scheduler — Controls execution timing; asyncScheduler, animationFrameScheduler, VirtualTime
  • Operators Overview — All operator families: creation, transformation, filtering, combination, error handling, multicasting, utility; first-order vs higher-order
  • Hot and Cold Observables — Precise definition via subscription side effects; temperature conversion (publish/defer); built-in temperature table; when to share
  • Custom Operators and pipe() — pipe() as composition operator; custom operators via pipe factory; parametric operators; low-level Observable constructor pattern
  • Operator Policies — Eight-Policy Framework — Start/Emit/Completion/State/Cancellation/Error/Output Type/Input Type; type chain rule; strongly typed Action/Reducer
  • Observable Internals — Building Observable from scratch; SafeObserver guarantees; operator pattern; Iterator + Observer synthesis; Observable vs Promise
  • combineLatest — Formal Rules — Five rules: initial sync, any-source trigger, latest retention, all-complete, error propagation; EMPTY trap; cold observable trap
  • shareReplay — Caching and Multicasting — HTTP caching pattern; refCount true/false; fixed TTL (windowTime); sliding TTL with defer; manual invalidation; error replay pitfall
  • RxJS Execution Phases — Plan phase (lazy blueprint) vs Execution phase (subscribe triggers); lossy vs non-lossy operators; scheduler as dispatcher bridge
  • Combination Operators — Complete Reference — Decision tree; per-operator reference: combineLatest, concat, forkJoin, merge, race, withLatestFrom, zip, pairwise, startWith; comparison table
  • FRP Concepts — Behaviors and Events — Classic FRP origins: Behavior (continuous, Time→A) vs Event (discrete, [(Time,A)]); fold-events-into-behavior = scan; inputs→combinators→outputs pattern; how RxJS maps to classic FRP
  • RxJS as a Dataflow Model — Observable pipelines as directed graphs; operators as nodes; Unix pipe philosophy; OperatorFunction as universal interface; higher-order operators as nested subgraphs
  • Stream Machine Calculus — 6 irreducible machines: sampleOn, recomputeLatest, reduceState, flattenWithPolicy, composeTopology, shareExecution; two vocabulary layers (teaching DSL + runtime DSL); complete operator→machine map; 8 classification axes
  • Higher-Order Operators — Complete Deep Dive — switchMap / mergeMap / concatMap / exhaustMap with marble diagrams; the *All variants; expand for recursion; error handling inside inners; memory leak patterns; decision guide by use case

History ​

RxJS version history and migration guides.

  • Timeline — From Rx.NET (2009) to RxJS 8; key people; version milestones
  • Erik Meijer — Mathematical foundations: the IEnumerable/IObservable dual, Raven Test origin, games as stream transformations, combineLatest stolen from spreadsheets
  • RxJS 5 → 6 Migration — The pipe() revolution; import path changes; rxjs-compat migration strategy
  • RxJS 6 → 7 Migration — TypeScript rewrite; lastValueFrom; throwError factory; share() config

Patterns ​

Proven solutions to recurring reactive programming problems.

  • Patterns Index — Quick reference recipes: search, polling, form submit, combining state, cleanup
  • Animations Pattern — animationFrameScheduler; velocity vs duration-based animations; defer for lazy timestamps; easing; tween with switchMap; higher-order reuse
  • Domain Operators Pattern — Wrap operator chains in business-named functions; layered architecture; testability; error isolation; anti-patterns
  • MVU Pattern — Model–View–Update (Elm-like): action→reducer→state→view with scan
  • Effects Pattern — NgRx-style Action In → Action Out; switchMap vs exhaustMap choice; error handling in effects
  • State Management — BehaviorSubject store; scan-based reducer; selectors; immutable updates
  • Error Handling — catchError inside inner; retry; exponential backoff; graceful degradation
  • State Machine Pattern — scan+action stream (MVU-style) and expand-based recursive approach; testing; composability vs XState

Architectures ​

Complete application architectures using RxJS.

  • Architectures Index — Comparison table; common principles; dependency graph
  • MVU Architecture — Full wiring: action$, reducer, state$, selectors, effects, view binding, cleanup
  • redux-observable — Epics pattern; RTK integration; comparison vs custom MVU
  • Event-Driven Architecture — Typed event bus; micro-frontend communication; request/response over bus
  • XState vs RxJS — Control graph vs dataflow graph; traffic light example; pedestrian button as the deciding test; when to use each

Testing ​

Testing strategies for Observable streams and RxJS-based systems.

  • Testing Index — Testing layers (reducer, operator, effect, state); vitest config; quick patterns
  • Marble Testing — Marble syntax; cold/hot Observables; expectObservable; testing switchMap vs mergeMap
  • TestScheduler — Virtual time; run() API; time(), cold(), hot(), expectSubscriptions; complete examples

Debugging ​

Techniques for diagnosing and fixing RxJS problems.

  • Debugging Index — Debugging checklist; tap workflow; subscription tracking; DevTools integration
  • tap Operator — Side effects and debugging; named tap helper; finalize; tap vs map
  • Common Mistakes — 13 most common RxJS pitfalls with diagnosis and fixes

Operations Log ​

See log for the chronological history of wiki changes.
